title: caprifoliaceae
intro: The caprifoliaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the honeysuckle or woodbine family. This family consists of about 300 species of plants, many of which are shrubs or woody vines. Caprifoliaceae includes various plants with showy flowers, such as honeysuckle, weigela, and fothergilla, often found in temperate climates. The family is characterized by its characteristic five-lobed calyx and tubular or bell-shaped corolla.
